creator: support@schavemaker.com
text:
I thought i had anwered this already.
But i guess the mail did not come through.

My smb.conf file.

[global]
workgroup = MYDOMAIN
netbios name = BEASTY
realm = MYDOMAIN.LOCAL
server string = Samba Server
security = ADS
interfaces = ale0

#idmap uid = 150000-550000
#idmap gid = 150000-550000
template shell = /usr/local/bin/bash
template homedir = /usr/home/%U
winbind use default domain = Yes
winbind cache time = 3600
winbind nested groups = yes
winbind separator = |
winbind enum users = yes
winbind enum groups = yes
winbind offline logon = yes

socket options = TCP_NODELAY IPTOS_LOWDELAY SO_RCVBUF=65536 SO_SNDBUF=65536
max protocol = SMB2
strict locking = no
strict sync = no
sync always = no
read raw = yes
min receivefile size=16384
use sendfile=true
aio read size = 1
aio write size = 1
aio write behind = true


obey pam restrictions = yes
read raw = yes
local master = no
domain logons = no
domain master = no
preferred master = no
dns proxy = no
username map = /usr/local/samba/usermap
# printers - configured to use CUPS and automatically load them
         load printers = yes
         printcap name = cups
         printing = cups
         cups options = Raw
#       show add printer wizard = no

# LOGGING
syslog only = Yes
log file = /var/log/samba/%m
loglevel = 5
debug level = 30
#max log size = 1000
syslog = 1

#============================ Share Definitions 
==============================

[homes]
         comment = Home Directories
         valid users = DOUBLE-L|%S
         read only = no
         browseable = no
         root preexec = /usr/local/bin/bash 
/usr/local/etc/samba/mkhomedir.sh %U

[share1]
         comment = Data Directory
         path = /mnt
         read only = no
         create mask = 0777
         directory mask = 0777


My windows 7 machine is not a member of the domain.
But the same issue is present on the Windows 2008 windows domain 
controller also.

I did set the debug level higher, but the logs stay the same.

regards
Johan Hendriks
